name: RSPO PC 2018 v1
description: RSPO Principles & Criteria 2018 — 8 principles covering legal compliance, environmental responsibility, social good practice, and commitment to continuous improvement for palm oil producers.

requirements.name: High Conservation Value Assessment (Criterion 5.2)
requirements.description: Ensure no primary forests or areas with High Conservation Values (HCV) are cleared or damaged. An HCV assessment has been completed and reviewed.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide the HCV assessment report and evidence of management plans for any identified HCV areas. Apply the RSPO-endorsed HCV methodology.

requirements.name: No Burning Policy (Criterion 5.5)
requirements.description: There is no use of fire for land preparation or replanting.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide the zero-burning policy document and evidence of its implementation (e.g., field inspection records, worker training records).

requirements.name: GHG Emissions Reporting (Criterion 5.6)
requirements.description: Monitor and report GHG emissions from palm oil production using the RSPO PalmGHG calculator.
requirements.guidanceText: Use the RSPO PalmGHG v4 calculator. Submit the completed PalmGHG output file as evidence. Report total net emissions in tCO2e per year.

requirements.name: No New Development on Peatland (Criterion 5.7)
requirements.description: No new plantings have occurred on peatland of any depth since November 2018.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide a peatland map of the supply base and a declaration confirming no new development on peatland since the RSPO 2018 P&C cutoff date.

requirements.name: POME Management Plan (Criterion 4.4)
requirements.description: An effluent treatment plan for POME is in place and complies with applicable discharge standards.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide the POME treatment system description, monthly effluent monitoring records, and evidence of compliance with national effluent discharge limits (e.g., DOE Malaysia EQA limits).

requirements.name: No Forced or Trafficked Labour (Criterion 6.13)
requirements.description: There is no use of forced, bonded, or trafficked labour.
requirements.guidanceText: Review employment contracts to confirm no recruitment fees, no retention of identity documents, and freedom to terminate employment. Audit foreign worker programmes if applicable.

requirements.name: No Child Labour (Criterion 6.7)
requirements.description: No child labour is used. Workers under 18 are not employed in hazardous conditions.
requirements.guidanceText: Confirm minimum working age (15 or higher per national law). Check age documentation for all workers. Maintain a register of young workers.

requirements.name: Grievance Mechanism (Criterion 6.3)
requirements.description: A documented grievance mechanism is in place and accessible to all workers and affected communities.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide evidence of the grievance procedure and records of grievances received and resolved during the reporting period.

requirements.name: Occupational Health & Safety Plan (Criterion 4.7)
requirements.description: An occupational health and safety (OHS) plan is in place and covers all workers on site.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide the current OHS policy, training records, accident log, and evidence of annual OHS review. Report the Lost-Time Injury Rate (LTIR).

requirements.name: Compliance with Laws (Criterion 2.1)
requirements.description: Compliance with all applicable local, national, and ratified international laws and regulations.
requirements.guidanceText: Maintain a legal register and provide compliance checks. Include evidence of current operating licences, environmental approvals, and resolution of any regulatory notices.
